fix: 在 ESM 相对路径 import 中添加 .js 扩展名

Node.js v22 ESM 严格模式要求相对路径导入必须包含完整文件扩展名（.js），否则运行时报 ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND。

- alerts.ts: '../services/alert-service' -> '../services/alert-service.js'
- encryption-service.ts: '../utils/jwt-secret' -> '../utils/jwt-secret.js'

fix(intent): static import for getDefaultLedger in registry.execute

The dynamic `await import("@/intent/instance")` in the resolution-
write path was hitting tsup's chunk-naming non-determinism: between
two builds the chunk hash changed, and a daemon running the older
build couldn't find the imported module:

  [ledger] resolution write failed for X/mesh-receiver:
  Cannot find module '/Users/.../dist/instance-BH5QFQ7I.js'

Static import resolves at module load — no runtime chunk lookup —

phase-1: cleanup orphaned in-flight ledger dispatches at daemon startup

Companion to the prior commit's resolution-on-completion wiring. When
the daemon restarts, any dispatched-and-unresolved decisions in the
ledger have stale state — the agent that was running died with the
previous process. Without writing a "canceled" resolution for them,
they sit in-flight forever and Inv-ActiveTaskSafety blocks every
re-dispatch on the same (project, subject).

fix(web): unbreak load-older history pagination in chat pane

Long conversations would stop loading older messages after one or two
trips to the top because several independent bugs compounded:

- The trigger relied on `useScroll`'s `directions.top`, which the
  browser stops emitting once `scrollTop === 0`; once a user pinned at
  the top, no further fetches fired. Replaced with a sentinel +
  `useIntersectionObserver` that fires regardless of scroll-event
  cadence, with a chained re-check so

docs(publishUtils): correct TENCENT_COS_ENDPOINT comment

Old comment said the endpoint should be the virtual-host-style URL with
the bucket already in it. That is wrong — when forcePathStyle=false the
S3 SDK automatically prepends the bucket as a host segment, so passing
in a bucket-prefixed endpoint produces a two-level host
(<bucket>.<bucket>.cos.accelerate.myqcloud.com) and the wildcard cert
*.cos.accelerate.myqcloud.com no longer matches, yielding
ERR_TLS_CERT_ALTNAME_INVALID. Encoded the e

phase-1: wire ledger resolutions through registry.execute

The 1b shadow soak surfaced an architectural gap: every dispatched
ledger decision sat in-flight forever because resolutions were never
recorded. Inv-ActiveTaskSafety became vacuously over-aggressive —
every same-slot re-dispatch deduped against an already-completed
prior dispatch. Of the first 25 divergences observed in production,
24 were false positives caused by this missing wiring.

This commit threads `intentRef = { eventId, decide

fix(curator): unify under auxiliary.curator (hermes model, dashboard) (#17868)

Voscko reported curator.auxiliary.provider/model was advertised in the
docs but ignored — the review fork read only model.provider/default. The
narrow fix would wire the one-off key through, but that leaves curator
as a parallel system: not in `hermes model` → auxiliary picker, not in
the dashboard Models tab, missing per-task base_url/api_key/timeout/
extra_body.

Unify curator with the rest of the aux task system s

fix(skill): match registry skill by fallback_name when parsed name differs from manifest

When an upstream registry repo changes the SKILL.md frontmatter
field after a skill was already installed, the manifest still stores the
old name. resolve_registry_skill_dir previously only matched by entry.id
and entry.name, causing RegistrySkillNotFound errors and skills being
skipped during list_installed / get_installed.

Add fallback_name (skill directory file name) as an additional matching
criterion

Update README to reflect changes in chatbot settings terminology

实现 Lovely Octopus AgentWorker 运行时

新增 AgentWorker，负责常驻 Agent 的任务调度、Agent DM 处理、任务上下文组装和运行中人类消息注入。Worker 复用外部注入的 LLMProvider、ToolRegistry 和现有 AgentLoop，不实现第二套 LLM streaming 或工具调用循环。

为团队任务补充 report_progress 和 request_approval 工具，支持任务进度落库、审批暂停、approve/reject 后恢复执行，以及 cancel 控制消息中断当前 AgentLoop。关键路径增加中文日志和注释，方便后续排查调度、注入和审批状态流转。

新增 AgentWorker 单测和 Phase 2 in-process E2E 测试，覆盖 @agent 路由消息落库、assigned task 执行、工具过滤、审批恢复、运行中补充消息只注入一次和取消中断。

fix(dispatcher): exclude "PR found" handoff from retry counter (#50)

A dev process that exits after producing a PR is forward progress
(the PR is handed to review), not a crash. The Step 4 retry-counter
regex was matching the Step 5 "Task appears to have crashed. PR found"
comment, so pre-existing PR-found handoffs plus real crashes pushed
RETRY_COUNT to MAX_RETRIES. The next cycle then marked the issue
`stalled` the moment the review agent legitimately sent it back to
`pending-dev` with blocki

Add turn-based EasyCall voice QA loop

Adds a Twilio Gather fallback so OpenClaw can speak, listen, continue a customer order conversation, and store transcript evidence when realtime voice-call is unstable.

The description field contained bare colons (e.g. 'Use when: (1)') which
YAML parsers interpret as nested mapping values, producing:

remove the offending colon after 'Use when'. The skill now loads cleanly
in Claude Code / Codex / any YAML-frontmatter-based skill system.

Chat attachments with non-ASCII filenames (e.g. Chinese) get sanitized
at upload time — `人人有虾.docx` is stored as `1777391026594_____.docx`.
Tools then receive only the original filename via '[Attachment] foo.docx'
and fail with 'file not found'.

fix(release): make publish-release.ps1 work under PowerShell 5.1

Native commands (npm, etc.) write informational messages like
'npm notice' to stderr. Under Windows PowerShell 5.1, native stderr is
wrapped into ErrorRecords; combined with $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
the script aborted on harmless notices before reaching tauri:build.

Switch to 'Continue' and add an Assert-ExitCode helper that explicitly
checks $LASTEXITCODE after each native command that we actually need to
gate on (npm ins

feat(dashboard): add --stop and --status flags (#17840)

`hermes dashboard` is a long-lived foreground server that users often
start and forget about, sometimes in a shell they've since closed.  We
didn't have a way to stop it — users had to find the PID manually.

Adds two lifecycle flags that reuse the same detection + termination
path the post-`hermes update` cleanup (PR #17832) uses:

  hermes dashboard --status
    List running hermes dashboard processes with PID + cmdline.
    Exit 0, info

agent_messages with status='ok' and rendered as a green Success badge,

fix(daemon): handle fsList error on broken symlink (#255) (#258)

* fix(daemon): handle fsList error on broken symlink (#255)

* fix(daemon): fix fsList error on broken symlink

The `fsList` handler throws a "Cannot read properties of null" error when encountering a broken symlink because `fs.stat().catch(() => null)` evaluates to null, but `.mtimeMs` is accessed unconditionally. Added optional chaining (`?.mtimeMs`) to fix this.

startsWith so the metadata block is found anywhere in the text.

新增 Team 模式纵向验证

新增 TeamVerticalIntegration 测试，用真实 AgentLoop 和真实 ToolRegistry 验证 Team 模式的最小执行链路。测试从 AgentRegistry、TaskQueue、TeamMessageStore、ProjectChannelStore 组装一个 assigned 任务，构造 agent soul、AGENTS 指令、任务上下文和 team_messages，然后通过 AgentLoop.run() 执行，并把结果写回 TaskQueue.completeTask。

这个测试刻意只 mock LLMProvider，不 mock AgentLoop 和 ToolRegistry，用来锁定后续 AgentWorker 的架构方向：Team 模式负责调度、消息持久化和上下文组装，底层执行复用现有 AgentLoop。测试同时验证 agent.yaml.tools 能映射到 AgentLoop.allowedTools，未允许的工具不会暴露给模型。

fix(db): bump 0056 journal when so drizzle-kit no longer skips it

The 0056 migration was generated with a real Date.now() timestamp
(2026-04-30), but 0054/0055 were authored with later integer-hour
timestamps in May. drizzle-kit migrate compares each entry's when
against the max created_at already in __drizzle_migrations and skips
anything not strictly newer, so 0056 was silently skipped on every
environment past the 0055 watermark — leaving file_key NOT NULL and
breaking external pass-through

fix(daemon): include stderr tail in ACP runtime error text (#454)

When an ACP runtime (hermes-agent, codex, etc.) reports `stopReason:
refusal|error` or throws a JSON-RPC error during `session/prompt`, the
child process is still alive — so the existing post-exit branch that
appends `stderrTail` never fires. The dispatcher then logs only the
opaque message (e.g. `acp error -32603: Internal error` or
`prompt stopped: refusal`) with no hint of the underlying cause.

Append the last 500 bytes of st

search and rerank which can exceed the previous 10s timeout shared
with general status queries. Introduce a dedicated
RUNTIME_KNOWLEDGE_TIMEOUT constant set to 60s to avoid premature
 timeouts on knowledge retrieval.

ther

fix(bin): work around libuv async assertion on Windows exit (#14)

* fix(bin): work around libuv async assertion on Windows exit

trueconf-setup crashes at the end of a successful run on Windows + Node
24.x with `assertion failed !(handle->flags & UV_HANDLE_CLOSING)` in
src/win/async.c. Root cause is the open Node issue nodejs/node#56645 —
Node calls `uv_async_send()` on a closing handle when `process.exit()`
short-circuits libuv teardown right after `fetch()`. PR
nodejs/node#61999 has been open

实现 TeamRouter 人类消息路由

本次提交新增 Lovely Octopus 的 TeamRouter，作为所有人类团队消息的确定性第一站。TeamRouter 会先把输入写入 TeamMessageStore，再按固定顺序处理控制命令、审批回复、@agent、#project、外部 chat 绑定和 coordinator 兜底，避免简单路由进入 LLM。

支持的路由能力包括：/task approve/reject/cancel 直接更新 TaskQueue；/pause、/resume 更新 AgentRegistry 中的 agent 状态；/project 绑定当前外部 chat 到项目频道；/status 返回基础状态；@agent 支持 name 和 aliases；#project 会匹配或创建 ProjectChannelStore 中的项目频道；无法确定目标时进入 coordinator。

TeamMessageStore 新增 routeMessage()，用于先落库后原地更新消息的最终 channel/project/task 目标。这样可以保留

SkillPackageResolver.persistScanOutcome built a fresh SkillEntity with only
id + scan fields, then called updateById. SkillEntity declares six columns
with @TableField(updateStrategy = FieldStrategy.ALWAYS) — name_zh, name_en,
config_json, source_code, skill_content, security_scan_result — so the
ALWAYS strategy emits UPDATE statements that write NULL to every one of
those columns not set on the partial entity.

fix(openclaw): chown secrets.json in fast tick to win the reload race

Pinchy writes /openclaw-secrets/secrets.json as uid 999 (the pinchy
user inside its container). OpenClaw's secret-provider resolver
requires owner == process uid (root) and rejects the reload with
SECRETS_RELOADER_DEGRADED otherwise.

The 30 s mtime watch loop in this script already chowns the file
back after a write — but the inotify-driven [reload] pipeline that
fires from a config change runs ~100 ms after Pinchy's
regener

fix(proxy): 4b — register agent in openclaw on internal POST

POST /api/internal/agents now calls registerAgentInOpenClaw after the
DB insert succeeds. Skills are resolved with this precedence:
  1. on-disk openclaw/workspaces/<slug>/agent-config.json (source of truth)
  2. widgetConfig.skills from the request body
  3. registerAgentInOpenClaw default ['website-api']

Failures are logged but do not fail the request: the row is already
persisted and the startup reconciler will pick it up.

supplies a replacement (agentId, outcome, reason?). The replay
substitutes that decision; every other decision

refactor(controller): modernize Go code with slices, maps, and cmp packages (#79)

Replace legacy `sort` package usage with modern Go 1.21+ alternatives:
- Use `slices.Sort()` instead of `sort.Ints()`
- Use `slices.SortFunc()` with `cmp.Compare()` instead of `sort.Slice()`
- Use `bytes.SplitSeq()` instead of `bytes.Split()` for iterator-based splitting
- Use `maps.Copy()` for merging manifest maps with preallocated capacity

These changes improve type safety, memory efficiency, and code readabil

Fix event loop blocking by cleaning up old plugin-runtime-deps

Problem: OpenClaw pods experiencing 20-40 second event loop delays due to
accumulated plugin-runtime-deps from multiple OpenClaw versions (1.5GB+).

Root cause: When OpenClaw upgrades versions, it downloads new plugin
dependencies but doesn't clean up old versions. Scanning hundreds of
thousands of files in old node_modules blocks the Node.js event loop.

Solution: Added automatic cleanup in entrypoint.sh that:
- Detects current Ope

v4.2.0a1: slice 2 — federation NATS push consumer

Closes the documented v4.2 federation gap. Replaces the per-peer
HTTP-poll loop (federation_worker_loop, GET /v1/federation/feed?since)
with a JetStream durable subscription per upstream peer. Memory
mutations propagate in real time instead of waiting on the 30-sec
poll cadence.

The HTTP poll path is preserved unchanged for backfill + safety —
peers fall back to polling if NATS is unreachable, and operators
can re-trigger backfill via the exist

Estimate Kubernetes spend by attributing each pod's share of its host
node's hourly price. Pod share = max(cpu_request / node_alloc_cpu,
mem_request / node_alloc_mem); pod cost = node_hourly * share. This is
the standard Kubecost-style model.

fix(word): paragraph effective.direction inherits from enclosing section bidi

The paragraph-direction resolver walked the style chain, numbering, table
style, and docDefaults but skipped the section. A document with section
<w:bidi/> set but no per-paragraph or per-style bidi therefore showed no
effective.direction at all, even though Word renders the page RTL.
Insert section-level fallback between numbering and docDefaults; src
points at /section[N] so callers can trace inheritance.

fix(word): comment Add validates author/initials/text for illegal XML chars before mutation

Without pre-validation a C0 control char (U+0001..U+001F minus tab/LF/CR)
in author/initials/text would let us append the comment to the comments
part, then explode at Save() — producing an orphaned comment with no
anchor in the document body (torn write). Reject up front.

fix(memory): use next_back instead of last on DoubleEndedIterator (#4143)

clippy::double_ended_iterator_last hard-fails CI under -D warnings.
The stamps adapter is `Vec::iter().filter_map(...)`, which is
DoubleEndedIterator, so next_back() yields the last stamped message
in O(k) without scanning the whole tail.

Regression from #4141 — the original PR's Quality job was failing on
this exact lint when it merged, but the merge went through anyway,
breaking every PR that auto-syncs against main.

fix(word): docDefaults.font.hAnsi Set handles canonical key

TrySetDocDefaults switch had no case for 'docdefaults.font.hansi', so
Set with the key Get returns silently no-op'd and reported the key as
unsupported. Add hansi/highansi case writing RunFonts.HighAnsi only,
matching the EastAsia / ComplexScript pattern.

fix(teams): override send_image_file for local image attachments

The gateway calls send_image_file() for locally cached images
(e.g. from image_gen tools). Without this override the base class
falls back to sending the file path as plain text. Delegate to
send_image() which already handles base64 encoding local paths.

fix(messages): backfill PG pod row on first message via shared sync service (#255)

Pods created via the Mongo-only POST /api/pods path (podController) have
no row in the PG pods table. The subsequent PGMessage.create from the
dual-DB messageController fails the messages.pod_id foreign key, so the
message silently lands in the Mongo fallback indefinitely — leaving the
pod in a degraded "single-DB" state that breaks reply threading,
findActivityHint, and the agent activity hint injection.

feat(onboard): detect Windows-host Ollama from WSL via host.docker.inernal (#2707)

<!-- markdownlint-disable MD041 -->
## Summary
When Ollama runs on the Windows host (a common WSL setup), the WSL
onboard CLI couldn't detect it because Ollama's default `127.0.0.1`
binding lives in a different network namespace from WSL. This adds a
`host.docker.internal` fallback probe so WSL users with Windows-host
Ollama (`OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0`) get auto-detection without manual
configuration.

fix(pptx): lang.ea / lang.cs rejected as UNSUPPORTED on shape and notes Set

prior alias `lang.{latin,ea,cs}` -> @lang silently collapsed to
last-write-wins, so a single Set call with all three keys dropped two
of the user's values without warning. Keep lang.latin as an alias for
`lang` (Word vocabulary parity), drop the lang.ea / lang.cs aliases
so they fall through to the unsupported list. Also accept lang.latin
on shape Ad

ci: pass WEBSITE_REPO_TOKEN to auto-update-branches script (#4142)

GitHub silently suppresses workflow runs for any commit pushed by the
default GITHUB_TOKEN to prevent recursion (documented behavior). The
auto-update job calls pulls.updateBranch every 6h, which produces a
merge commit authored as github-actions[bot]; that commit's push event
never fires `on: pull_request`, so the PR's CI rollup ends up empty
even though the PR is up to date with main.

Pass WEBSITE_REPO_TOKEN as github-token.

fix(anthropic): reactive recovery for OAuth 1M-context beta rejection (#17752)

Keep context-1m-2025-08-07 in OAuth requests by default so 1M-capable
subscriptions retain full context. When Anthropic rejects a request with
400 'long context beta is not yet available for this subscription',
disable the beta for the rest of the session, rebuild the client, and
retry once.

Addresses #17680 (thanks @JayGwod for the clean reproduction) without
forcing every OAuth user off the 1M context window.

fix(messages): pull _id from populated User in normalizeMongo (#253)

When .populate('userId') runs, m.userId is a populated Mongoose User
document, not an ObjectId. Calling .toString() on a Mongoose document
returns util.inspect output (\"{\n  _id: new ObjectId(\\\"...\\\"),\n
username: '...'\n}\"), which leaked into the user_id field of every
API response that hit the Mongo fallback path.

With the dev cluster's PG host unreachable (Aiven NXDOMAIN), every
message read goes through this fallbac
feat: sdd文档驱动开发落地

fix: sed in-place to avoid config write anomaly

The substitute_env() function was copying config to /tmp/, sed'ing
the temp, then cp'ing it back over the original. This replaced the
file (new inode), causing OpenClaw to detect a sha256 mismatch on
any subsequent config save — triggering 'missing-meta-before-write'
anomaly + constant config reloads that blocked the event loop.

Fix: sed -i directly on the original file. Same inode, no anomaly,
no spurious reloads → faster response times.

fix(word): Add r[N] return path skips comment-reference runs

The 8 sites in Add.Media/Add.Text/Add.Misc that build the returned r[N]
path were enumerating Elements<Run>() while navigation r[N] resolution
uses Descendants<Run>() filtered by GetFirstChild<CommentReference>() == null.

through the existing GetAllRuns helper so the index matches navigation.

fix(core): FormatEmu falls back to raw EMU for sub-cm precision

Small absolute EMU values (e.g. -100) round to '-0cm' under the '0.##cm'
formatter, losing all information on Get readback. When the cm-formatted
string would be '0' or '-0' for a non-zero EMU value, emit the raw EMU
integer instead — ParseEmu accepts unsuffixed integers, so Get/Set
round-trips preserve the original value. Zero stays as '0cm'.

jc-workers _run spawns the brain adapter with cwd=instance_dir, so a
fresh `claude -p` loads the instance CLAUDE.md and applies the standing
"spawn workers for substantive dev work" rule against its own prompt.
The worker then calls `jc workers spawn` instead of doing the work, the
sub-worker repeats, and the chain becomes unbounded — observed today in
rachel_zane: 40+ workers spawned in ~7 minutes on a single sender-approval

fix(vision): use HERMES_HOME-based cache dir instead of cwd (#17719)

vision_analyze used Path('./temp_vision_images') — a relative path that
resolved against cwd. Under Docker the image's WORKDIR is /opt/hermes,
which is root-owned and only chmoded a+rX (read + traversal). Since
#5811 landed (run as non-root hermes UID 10000, Apr 12), remote-URL
vision calls fail with PermissionError on mkdir.

Switch to get_hermes_dir('cache/vision', 'temp_vision_images'): resolves
to $HERMES_HOME/cache/vision

## Summary

Fixes #2674 — `nemoclaw onboard` hangs at "[3/8] Configuring inference
(NIM)" on macOS (and any host) when a local Ollama or vLLM port is
half-open or has a stalled listener.

The detection probes in `setupNim()` shelled out to `curl -sf
<localhost-url>` with no timeout, so `spawnSync` would block
indefinitely on a stuck TCP session, freezing the entire onboarding flow
with no recourse beyond Ctrl

curl is a ubiquitous tool both for users running ad-hoc commands inside
the container (debugging, health checks, quick HTTP probes) and for
agent workflows — many bundled skills and hub skills lean on curl for
HTTP calls, API exploration, and installer bootstrapping. Its absence
causes silent workflow failures with "curl: command not found" until
the user manually apt-installs it.

Add curl to the single apt-get install layer alongside the other base
ut

because the telemetry and Community-SaaS bootstrap modules co-locate

fix(word): strip BOM (U+FEFF) from font.{ascii,latin,ea,cs} input

Font names are token-like strings: a leading BOM produced by Windows
clipboard / shell quoting paths previously round-tripped into OOXML
as a literal U+FEFF byte glued to the typeface name (e.g.
<w:rFonts cs="﻿Arabic Typesetting"/>), which broke Word's font
lookup. SanitizeFontTokenInput now trims leading/trailing BOMs plus
surrounding whitespace before assignment to the rFonts attribute.

fix(word): empty font.{ascii,latin,ea,cs} value clears the slot

Previously `set ... --prop font.cs=` left a stub <w:rFonts cs=""/> in the
rPr and Get returned font.cs="". Empty values now clear the targeted
RunFonts attribute (and remove the rFonts element entirely when no
attribute remains), matching the empty-clears semantics of direction=.
Navigation read paths skip empty rFonts attribute values so legacy
documents carrying a stub do not surface ghost keys.

Add("sdt", type=text|...) on an inline SDT now mirrors AddRun's
rtl cascade: when the host paragraph carries pPr/bidi or paragraph-mark
rPr/rtl, the contentRun gains rPr/rtl. Previously the contentRun had no

feat(hub,daemon): S3 large-file end-to-end flow (#24)

Wires the spec's "Large File S3 Transfer" flow end-to-end. Read responses larger than the configured threshold get swapped for a presigned GET URL by the hub; large writes use the new `POST /files/upload-url` endpoint to PUT directly to S3, then a `FileRequest { write: FullWrite { s3_object_key } }` carries the key to the daemon, which fetches via a hub-injected presigned GET URL. Daemons stay S3-unaware: no AWS SDK, no credentials.

fix(plugin): remove over-strict character check from ensureAgentDir

The [a-z0-9_-]+ validation broke existing named-agent jobs whose
agent directories have uppercase letters, spaces, or other valid
filesystem characters. The resolve-under-agents/ check is the correct
and sufficient security boundary — it blocks path traversal regardless
of character set. Discord-generated keys are already safe via agentDirKey;
filesystem-derived job agent names pass through as-is, same as before.

fix(onboard): classify TLS certificate errors during sandbox create (#1936)

Add "tls_cert_mismatch" classification to classifySandboxCreateFailure()
so that TLS/certificate errors (e.g. "invalid peer certificate:
BadSignature") are detected instead of falling through to "unknown".

When this failure kind is detected, printSandboxCreateRecoveryHints()
now tells the user to re-trust the gateway certificate with `openshell
gateway trust -g nemoclaw` before resuming onboarding.

Closes #1933

fix(pptx): reset model3d render cache between HTML preview calls

_glbDataCache and _model3dCounter were process-global statics that never reset. Within a single render the cache correctly dedupes identical GLBs across slides, but on call N+1 the cache is still populated, so RenderModel3D skips emitting the <script>window._glbN='...'</script> data block — leaving the module script in the new HTML reading from a window variable that the receiver never saw.

Why: the dev image (Dockerfile.pinchy.dev) skips the production user
demotion (entrypoint.sh's `su -s /bin/sh pinchy`). Pinchy ran as root
in CI, masking the entire class of bugs where Pinchy (uid 999 in
production) can't read files OpenClaw writes as root. PR #195's
telegram-pairing.json mode 0600 bug went green in CI for the same
reason for months.

fix(telegram): allow Pinchy to read OpenClaw's pairing file

as root:0600. Pinchy runs as uid 999 in production (entrypoint.sh demotes
via 'su -s /bin/sh pinchy'), so readFileSync throws EACCES and the bare
catch{} in resolvePairingCode silently returns { found: false }. Users
saw "Invalid or expired pairing code" while the file actually contained
the matching code.

fail to install runtime deps with EACCES — including 'anthropic' and
'telegram', so the gateway loads with no auth providers and no channel
polling.

fix(plugin): always probe live gateway model for TUI footer (#2661)

## Summary

After a runtime `openshell inference set`, the OpenClaw TUI footer
continued showing the onboard-time model because the plugin only read
the model from the on-disk onboard config (which is never updated at
runtime). This fix makes the plugin always probe the live gateway
inference state at registration time and prefer that result for both the
banner display and registered provider model.

## Related Issue

Fixes #26

fix(ci): use release.published instead of released

GitHub may only emit the `published` event (not `released`) when a
pre-existing draft release is publicly released — causing the distribution
workflow to be silently skipped for affected releases.

Switch to `published` and add an explicit prerelease guard on the job
so prereleases are still filtered out.

fix(workflows): scope agent.execute by workflowRunId chatId

The workflow dispatcher's `agents.execute` shim was calling
registry.execute without context, so every workflow turn for a given
agent collided in `<agentId>:api:default:<day>.json`. Concurrent runs
bled context across each other; the per-run "single conversation"
guarantee was effectively broken.

"workflow" }` so each run gets its own session bucket. Falls back to

Refactor/persistence changes

fix(portability): make fcntl optional for Windows

fcntl is POSIX-only — on Windows the import would crash setup.py at
load time. Wrap the import in try/except and skip flock calls when
fcntl is unavailable. The skill is designed for one agent per
workspace at a time so concurrent writers are extremely rare; users
who need real Windows locking can patch _file_lock with portalocker.

fix(chat): keep composer input enabled during reconnects

The composer input was disabled whenever chatStatus.kind was 'unavailable'
or 'starting'. If the WebSocket dropped mid-sentence, the textarea locked
and the user lost their typing flow — frustrating, especially with the
gateway-restart cascade triggered by every settings save.

Typing should always be allowed. Submission is what's gated on connection
state, and that's already handled by ComposerAction's sendAllowed check.

fix(telemetry): remove DO_NOT_TRACK opt-out across plugin + CI (#64)

* fix(telemetry): remove DO_NOT_TRACK opt-out across plugin + CI

DO_NOT_TRACK is no longer honored as an AxonFlow telemetry opt-out
because it is commonly inherited from host tools and developer
environments, which makes it an unreliable expression of user intent.
AXONFLOW_TELEMETRY=off remains the canonical opt-out and is now the
only one.
